summary: IP2.A3 (Firestore rules deny client write to hand-result fields) does not pass against the default-deployed artifact (client-dealer mode). The assertion as literally written passes only after the user opts into cloud-functions mode.

evidence.expected: decisions/technical-discovery/sections-v1.json#inflection_resolutions[ip_id=IP2].machine_checkable_assertions[id=IP2.A3].expected_result — 'only the Cloud Function's service-account identity can update those fields'

proposed_resolution: Per dev-001's documented tradeoff, the relaxation is intentional and surfaced in user-visible docs. Two paths: (i) amend IP2.A3 to be mode-conditional ('In cloud-functions mode: only service account writes; in client-dealer mode: dealer client writes, confidentiality enforced via /player_hole_cards/{uid} per-player subcollection'); (ii) carry into the Uncertainty Manifest at delivery time. Recommend (ii) for this build (don't rewrite assertions post-hoc) and (i) for future architecture (assertions should express mode-conditional behavior when dual-path is the chosen architecture).
notes_for_arbiter: Medium severity; this is a documented dual-path tradeoff with honest user-facing disclosure, not a silent regression. CV should verify both modes are reachable per their respective claims. Architecture-level question — not a routing decision.
